title: Exercises: Decoherence
description: Practice modeling and analyzing decoherence in quantum systems

exercises.title: Amplitude Damping Channel
exercises.description: Implement the amplitude damping channel using Kraus operators and verify its properties: it takes |1> to |0> and preserves |0>.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def amplitude_damping_kraus(gamma):
    """
    Return Kraus operators for amplitude damping.
    
    Args:
        gamma: Damping parameter (0 to 1)
    
    Returns:
        Tuple of (K0, K1) Kraus operators
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

def apply_channel(rho, kraus_ops):
    """
    Apply quantum channel defined by Kraus operators.
    
    Args:
        rho: Input density matrix
        kraus_ops: List of Kraus operators
    
    Returns:
        Output density matrix
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

def verify_trace_preserving(kraus_ops):
    """
    Verify that sum of K_i^dag @ K_i = I.
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

# Test
for gamma in [0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0]:
    K0, K1 = amplitude_damping_kraus(gamma)
    
    # Apply to |1> state
    rho_1 = np.array([[0, 0], [0, 1]], dtype=complex)
    rho_out = apply_channel(rho_1, [K0, K1])
    
    print(f"gamma = {gamma:.2f}: P(|0>) = {np.real(rho_out[0,0]):.4f}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def amplitude_damping_kraus(gamma):
    """
    Return Kraus operators for amplitude damping.
    """
    K0 = np.array([[1, 0], [0, np.sqrt(1 - gamma)]])
    K1 = np.array([[0, np.sqrt(gamma)], [0, 0]])
    return K0, K1

def apply_channel(rho, kraus_ops):
    """
    Apply quantum channel defined by Kraus operators.
    """
    rho_out = np.zeros_like(rho)
    for K in kraus_ops:
        rho_out += K @ rho @ K.conj().T
    return rho_out

def verify_trace_preserving(kraus_ops):
    """
    Verify that sum of K_i^dag @ K_i = I.
    """
    total = sum(K.conj().T @ K for K in kraus_ops)
    return np.allclose(total, np.eye(len(total)))

# Test
print("Amplitude damping on |1> state:")
for gamma in [0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0]:
    K0, K1 = amplitude_damping_kraus(gamma)
    
    # Apply to |1> state
    rho_1 = np.array([[0, 0], [0, 1]], dtype=complex)
    rho_out = apply_channel(rho_1, [K0, K1])
    
    tp = verify_trace_preserving([K0, K1])
    print(f"gamma = {gamma:.2f}: P(|0>) = {np.real(rho_out[0,0]):.4f}, Trace-preserving: {tp}")
hints: Channel: rho' = sum_i K_i @ rho @ K_i^dagger

exercises.title: T1 and T2 Relationship
exercises.description: Implement a function that calculates the pure dephasing time T_phi from measured T1 and T2 values, and vice versa. Verify the constraint T2 <= 2*T1.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def calculate_t_phi(T1, T2):
    """
    Calculate pure dephasing time from T1 and T2.
    
    Uses: 1/T2 = 1/(2*T1) + 1/T_phi
    
    Returns:
        T_phi value, or np.inf if no pure dephasing
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

def calculate_t2(T1, T_phi):
    """
    Calculate T2 from T1 and T_phi.
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

def is_valid_t2(T1, T2):
    """
    Check if T2 value is physically valid given T1.
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

# Test cases
print("Testing T1/T2/T_phi relationships:")
print("-" * 50)

test_cases = [
    (100, 50),   # Typical case
    (100, 100),  # No pure dephasing
    (100, 200),  # Should be T_phi limited
    (100, 250),  # Invalid! T2 > 2*T1
]

for T1, T2 in test_cases:
    valid = is_valid_t2(T1, T2)
    if valid:
        T_phi = calculate_t_phi(T1, T2)
        print(f"T1={T1}, T2={T2}: Valid, T_phi={T_phi:.1f}")
    else:
        print(f"T1={T1}, T2={T2}: INVALID (T2 > 2*T1)")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def calculate_t_phi(T1, T2):
    """
    Calculate pure dephasing time from T1 and T2.
    """
    # 1/T2 = 1/(2*T1) + 1/T_phi
    # 1/T_phi = 1/T2 - 1/(2*T1)
    
    contribution_from_t1 = 1 / (2 * T1)
    dephasing_rate = 1/T2 - contribution_from_t1
    
    if dephasing_rate <= 0:
        return np.inf  # No pure dephasing
    
    return 1 / dephasing_rate

def calculate_t2(T1, T_phi):
    """
    Calculate T2 from T1 and T_phi.
    """
    # 1/T2 = 1/(2*T1) + 1/T_phi
    if T_phi == np.inf:
        return 2 * T1
    
    rate = 1/(2*T1) + 1/T_phi
    return 1 / rate

def is_valid_t2(T1, T2):
    """
    Check if T2 value is physically valid given T1.
    """
    return T2 <= 2 * T1

# Test cases
print("Testing T1/T2/T_phi relationships:")
print("-" * 50)

test_cases = [
    (100, 50),   # Typical case
    (100, 100),  # Some pure dephasing
    (100, 200),  # T2 = 2*T1, no pure dephasing
    (100, 250),  # Invalid! T2 > 2*T1
]

for T1, T2 in test_cases:
    valid = is_valid_t2(T1, T2)
    if valid:
        T_phi = calculate_t_phi(T1, T2)
        if T_phi == np.inf:
            print(f"T1={T1}, T2={T2}: Valid, T_phi=inf (no pure dephasing)")
        else:
            print(f"T1={T1}, T2={T2}: Valid, T_phi={T_phi:.1f}")
    else:
        print(f"T1={T1}, T2={T2}: INVALID (T2 > 2*T1)")

print("\nVerify round-trip:")
T1, T_phi = 100, 200
T2_calc = calculate_t2(T1, T_phi)
T_phi_back = calculate_t_phi(T1, T2_calc)
print(f"T1={T1}, T_phi={T_phi} -> T2={T2_calc:.1f} -> T_phi={T_phi_back:.1f}")

exercises.title: Ramsey Experiment Simulation
exercises.description: Simulate a Ramsey fringe experiment and extract T2* from the decay envelope. Include both coherent oscillations (from detuning) and incoherent decay.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def ramsey_signal(times, T2_star, detuning, amplitude=1.0, offset=0.5):
    """
    Calculate Ramsey fringe signal.
    
    P(|0>) = offset + amplitude * exp(-t/T2*) * cos(detuning * t)
    
    Args:
        times: Array of wait times
        T2_star: Dephasing time
        detuning: Frequency detuning (rad/s)
        amplitude: Fringe amplitude
        offset: DC offset
    
    Returns:
        Array of P(|0>) values
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

def fit_t2_star(times, signal):
    """
    Extract T2* from Ramsey data by fitting the envelope.
    
    Returns:
        Fitted T2* value
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

# Generate synthetic data
T2_true = 50e-6  # 50 us
detuning = 2 * np.pi * 200e3  # 200 kHz
times = np.linspace(0, 200e-6, 200)

# Add some noise
np.random.seed(42)
signal = ramsey_signal(times, T2_true, detuning)
signal_noisy = signal + 0.02 * np.random.randn(len(times))

# Fit and compare
T2_fit = fit_t2_star(times, signal_noisy)
print(f"True T2*: {T2_true*1e6:.1f} us")
print(f"Fitted T2*: {T2_fit*1e6:.1f} us")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np
from scipy.optimize import curve_fit

def ramsey_signal(times, T2_star, detuning, amplitude=0.5, offset=0.5):
    """
    Calculate Ramsey fringe signal.
    """
    envelope = np.exp(-times / T2_star)
    oscillation = np.cos(detuning * times)
    return offset + amplitude * envelope * oscillation

def fit_t2_star(times, signal):
    """
    Extract T2* from Ramsey data by fitting the envelope.
    """
    # Get envelope by taking absolute value of Hilbert transform
    # or by fitting peaks
    
    def fit_func(t, T2, detuning, amp, offset):
        return offset + amp * np.exp(-t/T2) * np.cos(detuning * t)
    
    # Initial guesses
    p0 = [50e-6, 2*np.pi*200e3, 0.5, 0.5]
    
    try:
        popt, _ = curve_fit(fit_func, times, signal, p0=p0, 
                           bounds=([1e-9, 0, 0, 0], [1e-3, 1e8, 1, 1]))
        return popt[0]
    except:
        # Fallback: simple envelope fitting
        envelope = np.abs(signal - 0.5)
        log_env = np.log(envelope + 1e-10)
        slope = np.polyfit(times, log_env, 1)[0]
        return -1/slope

# Generate synthetic data
T2_true = 50e-6  # 50 us
detuning = 2 * np.pi * 200e3  # 200 kHz
times = np.linspace(0, 200e-6, 200)

# Add some noise
np.random.seed(42)
signal = ramsey_signal(times, T2_true, detuning)
signal_noisy = signal + 0.02 * np.random.randn(len(times))

# Fit and compare
T2_fit = fit_t2_star(times, signal_noisy)
print(f"True T2*: {T2_true*1e6:.1f} us")
print(f"Fitted T2*: {T2_fit*1e6:.1f} us")
print(f"Error: {abs(T2_fit - T2_true)/T2_true * 100:.1f}%")
hints: Fit to extract T2* from envelope decay

exercises.title: Echo Sequence Simulation
exercises.description: Simulate a spin echo sequence and compare its coherence preservation to a simple Ramsey experiment. Show how the echo refocuses slow noise.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def simulate_ramsey_with_noise(T2_intrinsic, T2_star, total_time, n_realizations=100):
    """
    Simulate Ramsey experiment with quasi-static noise.
    
    T2_star < T2_intrinsic due to slow noise.
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

def simulate_echo_with_noise(T2_intrinsic, T2_star, total_time, n_realizations=100):
    """
    Simulate spin echo experiment.
    
    Echo should refocus slow noise, recovering T2_intrinsic.
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

# Compare Ramsey vs Echo
T2_intrinsic = 100e-6  # True T2
T2_star = 20e-6        # T2* limited by slow noise

print("Ramsey vs Echo comparison:")
print("-" * 50)
for t in [10e-6, 30e-6, 50e-6, 100e-6]:
    ramsey_fidelity = simulate_ramsey_with_noise(T2_intrinsic, T2_star, t)
    echo_fidelity = simulate_echo_with_noise(T2_intrinsic, T2_star, t)
    print(f"t = {t*1e6:5.0f} us: Ramsey = {ramsey_fidelity:.3f}, Echo = {echo_fidelity:.3f}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def simulate_ramsey_with_noise(T2_intrinsic, T2_star, total_time, n_realizations=100):
    """
    Simulate Ramsey experiment with quasi-static noise.
    """
    # Slow noise causes dephasing with T2_star
    # Intrinsic noise causes additional dephasing with T2_intrinsic
    
    # Combined decay
    visibility = np.exp(-total_time / T2_star)
    
    return 0.5 + 0.5 * visibility

def simulate_echo_with_noise(T2_intrinsic, T2_star, total_time, n_realizations=100):
    """
    Simulate spin echo experiment.
    
    Echo refocuses quasi-static noise, recovering T2_intrinsic.
    """
    # Echo refocuses the slow noise (T2_star contribution)
    # Only intrinsic dephasing remains
    
    visibility = np.exp(-total_time / T2_intrinsic)
    
    return 0.5 + 0.5 * visibility

def simulate_with_microscopic_model(T2_intrinsic, noise_sigma, total_time, 
                                     use_echo=False, n_realizations=1000):
    """
    More detailed simulation with explicit quasi-static noise.
    """
    # Each realization has a different quasi-static frequency offset
    # This offset is constant during one experiment
    
    phases = []
    
    for _ in range(n_realizations):
        # Sample quasi-static noise (frequency offset)
        delta_omega = np.random.normal(0, noise_sigma)
        
        if use_echo:
            # Echo: phase accumulates for t/2, then pi pulse reverses it
            # Net phase from quasi-static noise = 0
            phase_slow = 0
        else:
            # Ramsey: phase accumulates for full time
            phase_slow = delta_omega * total_time
        
        # Intrinsic dephasing (random per measurement)
        phase_intrinsic = np.random.normal(0, np.sqrt(total_time / T2_intrinsic))
        
        total_phase = phase_slow + phase_intrinsic
        phases.append(np.cos(total_phase))
    
    # Average over realizations
    visibility = np.mean(phases)
    return 0.5 + 0.5 * visibility

# Compare Ramsey vs Echo
T2_intrinsic = 100e-6  # True T2
T2_star = 20e-6        # T2* limited by slow noise

print("Ramsey vs Echo comparison:")
print("-" * 50)
for t in [10e-6, 30e-6, 50e-6, 100e-6]:
    ramsey_fidelity = simulate_ramsey_with_noise(T2_intrinsic, T2_star, t)
    echo_fidelity = simulate_echo_with_noise(T2_intrinsic, T2_star, t)
    improvement = echo_fidelity / ramsey_fidelity if ramsey_fidelity > 0.5 else 1
    print(f"t = {t*1e6:5.0f} us: Ramsey = {ramsey_fidelity:.3f}, Echo = {echo_fidelity:.3f}")

print("\nKey insight: Echo recovers T2_intrinsic by refocusing slow noise!")
hints: Echo works because slow noise is constant during the sequence

exercises.title: Decoherence of Entangled States
exercises.description: Analyze how local decoherence affects entangled states differently from product states. Compare the decay of a Bell state to two independent qubits.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def apply_local_dephasing(rho, gamma1, gamma2):
    """
    Apply independent dephasing to each qubit of a 2-qubit state.
    
    Args:
        rho: 4x4 density matrix
        gamma1, gamma2: Dephasing parameters for each qubit
    
    Returns:
        Dephased density matrix
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

def bell_state_fidelity(rho):
    """
    Calculate fidelity with Bell state |00> + |11>.
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

def product_state_fidelity(rho):
    """
    Calculate fidelity with product state |++>.
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

# Create Bell state and product state
bell = np.array([1, 0, 0, 1]) / np.sqrt(2)
rho_bell = np.outer(bell, bell.conj())

plus = np.array([1, 1]) / np.sqrt(2)
rho_plus_plus = np.outer(np.kron(plus, plus), np.kron(plus, plus).conj())

# Compare decoherence
print("Dephasing effect on Bell vs Product state:")
print("-" * 50)
for gamma in [0, 0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.8]:
    rho_bell_deph = apply_local_dephasing(rho_bell, gamma, gamma)
    rho_prod_deph = apply_local_dephasing(rho_plus_plus, gamma, gamma)
    
    f_bell = bell_state_fidelity(rho_bell_deph)
    f_prod = product_state_fidelity(rho_prod_deph)
    
    print(f"gamma = {gamma:.1f}: Bell fidelity = {f_bell:.4f}, Product fidelity = {f_prod:.4f}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def apply_local_dephasing(rho, gamma1, gamma2):
    """
    Apply independent dephasing to each qubit of a 2-qubit state.
    """
    Z = np.array([[1, 0], [0, -1]])
    I = np.eye(2)
    
    # Dephasing on qubit 1: rho -> (1-gamma1)*rho + gamma1*(Z1 @ rho @ Z1)
    Z1 = np.kron(Z, I)
    rho = (1 - gamma1) * rho + gamma1 * (Z1 @ rho @ Z1)
    
    # Dephasing on qubit 2: rho -> (1-gamma2)*rho + gamma2*(Z2 @ rho @ Z2)
    Z2 = np.kron(I, Z)
    rho = (1 - gamma2) * rho + gamma2 * (Z2 @ rho @ Z2)
    
    return rho

def bell_state_fidelity(rho):
    """
    Calculate fidelity with Bell state |00> + |11>.
    """
    bell = np.array([1, 0, 0, 1]) / np.sqrt(2)
    return np.real(bell.conj() @ rho @ bell)

def product_state_fidelity(rho):
    """
    Calculate fidelity with product state |++>.
    """
    plus = np.array([1, 1]) / np.sqrt(2)
    plus_plus = np.kron(plus, plus)
    return np.real(plus_plus.conj() @ rho @ plus_plus)

# Create Bell state and product state
bell = np.array([1, 0, 0, 1]) / np.sqrt(2)
rho_bell = np.outer(bell, bell.conj())

plus = np.array([1, 1]) / np.sqrt(2)
rho_plus_plus = np.outer(np.kron(plus, plus), np.kron(plus, plus).conj())

# Compare decoherence
print("Dephasing effect on Bell vs Product state:")
print("-" * 55)
print(f"{'gamma':^8} | {'Bell Fidelity':^15} | {'Product Fidelity':^15}")
print("-" * 55)

for gamma in [0, 0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.8]:
    rho_bell_deph = apply_local_dephasing(rho_bell, gamma, gamma)
    rho_prod_deph = apply_local_dephasing(rho_plus_plus, gamma, gamma)
    
    f_bell = bell_state_fidelity(rho_bell_deph)
    f_prod = product_state_fidelity(rho_prod_deph)
    
    print(f"{gamma:^8.1f} | {f_bell:^15.4f} | {f_prod:^15.4f}")

print("\nKey insight: Bell state is more robust to local Z dephasing!")
print("This is because |00> + |11> is an eigenstate of Z1*Z2.")
hints: Apply dephasing channel to each qubit independently
Bell state |00>+|11> is eigenstate of Z1*Z2
